projectTitle: AdvertEyes - OOH Advertising Internal Operations Platform
clientContext: Billboard, truckside (vehicle wraps), and digital (DOOH screens) advertising company. Project is a custom internal business platform to improve operations, manage inventory, organize campaigns, and provide a clean dashboard for internal teams.
projectScope: Custom internal/business platform (not SaaS) with admin dashboard, user management, role-based access control, REST APIs, database-driven workflows, and responsive React frontend.
featureRichnessInterpretation: Full, production-quality app with several real working features; hero is a multi-view module (Campaign Operations Dashboard); keep scope fenced and shippable within demo constraints.

heroFeature.name: Campaign Operations Dashboard
heroFeature.description: Inventory management (map/table views), campaign bookings with conflict detection, KPI tiles, filterable reports/tables, role-based access control gates what users see and do.
core: Inventory units (billboards, truck-side wraps, DOOH screens) with location, size, type, availability, base rate
Clients/advertisers and their campaigns (flights with start/end, budget, assigned inventory, creative)
Bookings linking campaigns to inventory over date ranges with conflict detection
User roles (Admin, Sales, Ops/Installer, Client-viewer) with RBAC protecting routes and views
Dashboard KPI tiles and filterable reports

supportingFeatures.name: Weather & Install-Risk Integration
supportingFeatures.description: Open-Meteo API (free, no key required) displays weather/install-risk panels per billboard location. Weather drives real OOH decisions: dayparting, install scheduling (no vinyl in rain/high wind), impression estimates. Flags campaigns/installs at risk.

supportingFeatures.name: User & Role Management
supportingFeatures.description: Demo login seeded per role (Admin, Sales, Ops, Client-viewer). Protected routes, token-based auth, role-gating on dashboard views/operations.

backend: Database (PostgreSQL, MongoDB, or SQLite for demo)
niceToHave: Experience with internal tools and admin dashboards
Experience with CRM, payment, email, or third-party integrations
Experience with AWS, Docker, CI/CD, or deployment workflows

postingRequiredAnswers: Experience with custom web applications — demonstrated in cover letter and app architecture
Examples of admin dashboards/internal tools built — the Campaign Operations Dashboard is the anchor
Strongest frontend and backend stack — React + TypeScript (FE), Node + Express + TypeScript (BE)
API integration experience — Open-Meteo weather integration + internal REST APIs for inventory/campaigns/bookings
Availability this week — can start immediately (demo is production-ready)

postingConstraints.rate: Hourly, willing to pay higher for experienced

explicitRequirementsFromPosting: Build and improve custom web application features
Create and enhance admin dashboard functionality
Implement user management and role-based access control
Build secure backend APIs and database-driven workflows
Connect third-party APIs and business tools (Open-Meteo)
Create forms, filters, tables, reports, and dashboard views
Improve application performance and user experience
Work with authentication, authorization, and protected routes
Support deployment and final project handoff
Document completed work and technical decisions

backendinforcement.approach: Node.js + Express.js service (TypeScript) via Surface runner, registered as a persistent service, bridged through Flask at api.michaelwegter.com/adverteyes
backendinforcement.fallback: If Surface Node build proves flaky within budget: seed realistic data in-frontend, call Open-Meteo directly from browser, document the Node/TS architecture as intended backend
backendinforcement.seed: Demo database (SQLite or JSON store) with sample inventory (billboards, trucks, DOOH screens), campaigns, bookings, users per role

designDirection.theme: Confident operations-tool aesthetic: bold, visual, high-contrast, map-forward. Professional but strong.
designDirection.constraint: NOT michaelwegter.com's dark gallery theme, mustard/cyan palette, or portfolio styling
designDirection.research: OOH industry cues (Lamar, Clear Channel Outdoor, OUTFRONT) pulled by researcher
designDirection.deliverables: Exact palette (with hex), typography, mood, and layout style set by planner

acceptanceCriteria: Demo builds and loads with no console errors on first paint
Campaign Operations Dashboard is functional with inventory, campaigns, bookings, and RBAC views
Open-Meteo weather integration displays for billboard locations
User login with demo accounts seeded per role (Admin, Sales, Ops, Client-viewer)
Protected routes enforce RBAC; each role sees appropriate views/operations
REST API backend (Node + Express + TS) is live and bridged at api.michaelwegter.com/adverteyes
Demo URL (/demos/adverteyes/) resolves locally; after deploy /work-samples/adverteyes returns 200
No em/en dashes in any prose (cover letter, one-pager, deck)
Demo link appears in cover letter and deck
Design is cohesive, bespoke, and fit for OOH industry (not portfolio look)
All explicit posting requirements are addressed in cover letter and represented in demo or deck
